---
title: "TBEM 2026"
format: html
editor: visual
---

Load packages and data sets

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(fishualize)
library(patchwork)
library(sf)
library(gstat)
library(maps)
library(vegan)

setwd("C:/Users/Owner/OneDrive/Documents/R Coding/DECORATE/TBEM 2026")

biobox<- read_csv(file = "data/2025 Bioboxes.csv", col_names = TRUE)
view(biobox)
oysters<- read_csv(file = "data/2025 Oyster Data.csv", col_names = TRUE)
view(oysters)
hydro<- read_csv(file = "data/2025 Hydro.csv", col_names = TRUE)
view(hydro)
hydro_maps<- read_csv(file = "data/2025 hydro maps.csv", col_names = TRUE)
view(hydro_maps)
```

Data Wrangling

```{r}
biobox.summary <- biobox %>%
  drop_na(total_length_mm, stand_length_mm, weight_g) %>%
  filter(species_type == "fish") %>%
  group_by( site, species_type, biobox_ID) %>%
  summarize(n = sum(number_individuals), biomass = sum(weight_g), size = mean(total_length_mm)) %>%
  ungroup()

oysters.dredge <- oysters %>%
  filter(extraction_type == "Dredge",
         oyster_mortality == "live") %>%
  group_by(site) %>%
  summarize(mean_live = mean(oyster_total_count, na.rm = TRUE))


hydro.sum <- hydro %>%
  group_by(site) %>%
  summarize(temp = mean(water_temp_C),
            do = mean(DO_mgL),
            sal = mean(sal_ppt))

```

Species Richness

```{r}
richness <- biobox %>%
  group_by(site) %>%
  summarise(richness = n_distinct(species))

richness.hydro <- richness %>%
  inner_join(hydro.sum)

richness.counts<- richness %>%
  inner_join(oysters.dredge)

richness.plot<- 
  ggplot(richness.hydro, aes(x = site, y = richness, color = site)) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = site, xend = site, y = 0, yend = richness),
               color = "gray70") +
  geom_point(size = 4) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "Site",
    y = "Species Richness"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        legend.position = "none")
ggsave(richness.plot, file = "plots/richnessplot.png", width = 10.5, height =6)
richness.sal.plot<- ggplot(richness.hydro, aes(x = sal, y = richness)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "#CE7DA5") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "#CE7DA5", fill = "#CE7DA5") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "Mean Salinity",
    y = "Species Richness"
  ) 

richness.temp.plot<- ggplot(richness.hydro, aes(x = temp, y = richness)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "#CE7DA5") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "#CE7DA5", fill = "#CE7DA5") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "Mean Temperature",
    y = "Species Richness"
  )
richness.counts.plot<- ggplot(richness.counts, aes(x = mean_live, y = richness)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "#CE7DA5") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "#CE7DA5", fill = "#CE7DA5") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "Mean live oyster count",
    y = "Species Richness"
  )
richness.hydro.count.plots<- richness.sal.plot+richness.temp.plot+richness.counts.plot
ggsave(richness.hydro.count.plots, file = "plots/richness.hydro.count.plots.png", width = 10.5, height = 6)
```

Species Diversity

```{r}
diversity <- biobox %>%
  group_by(site) %>%
  summarise(diversity = diversity(number_individuals, index = "shannon"))

diversity.hydro <- diversity %>%
  inner_join(hydro.sum)

diversity.counts<- diversity %>%
  inner_join(oysters.dredge)

diversity.sal.plot<- ggplot(diversity.hydro, aes(x = sal, y = diversity)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "#F1BF98") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "#F1BF98", fill = "#F1BF98") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "Mean Salinity",
    y = "Shannon-Wiener Diversity Index"
  ) 

diversity.temp.plot<- ggplot(diversity.hydro, aes(x = temp, y = diversity)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "#F1BF98") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "#F1BF98", fill = "#F1BF98") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "Mean Temperature",
    y = "Shannon-Wiener Diversity Index"
  )
diversity.counts.plot<- ggplot(diversity.counts, aes(x = mean_live, y = diversity)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "#F1BF98") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "#F1BF98", fill = "#F1BF98") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "Mean live oyster count",
    y = "Shannon-Wiener Diversity Index"
  )
diversity.hydro.count.plots<- diversity.sal.plot+diversity.temp.plot+diversity.counts.plot
ggsave(diversity.hydro.count.plots, file = "plots/diversity.hydro.count.plots.png", width = 10.5, height = 6)

```

Fish Community Metrics

```{r}
biobox.dredge <- 
  left_join(biobox.summary, oysters.dredge, by = "site")
view(biobox.dredge)
biobox.hydro <- 
  left_join(biobox.summary, hydro.sum, by = "site")

#statistics - linear models, log transformed
mod_abu <- lm(log(n + 1) ~ mean_live, data = biobox.dredge)
summary(mod_abu) #not significant

mod_biomass <- lm(log(biomass + 1) ~ mean_live, data = biobox.dredge)
summary(mod_biomass) #not significant


mod_size <- lm(size ~ mean_live, data = biobox.dredge)
summary(mod_size) #not significant


biobox.dredge.plot.abu <- ggplot(data = biobox.dredge, aes(x = mean_live, 
                                                           y = log(n+1))) +
  geom_point(color = "#5EB1BF") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "#5EB1BF", fill = "#5EB1BF") +
  theme_classic() +
  xlab("Mean live oyster count") +
  ylab("Log(Mean fish abundance + 1)") +
  theme_light()

biobox.dredge.plot.biomass <- ggplot(data = biobox.dredge, 
  aes(x = mean_live, y = log(biomass +1))) +
  geom_point(color = "#5EB1BF") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "#5EB1BF", fill = "#5EB1BF") +
  theme_classic() +
  xlab("Mean live oyster count") +
  ylab("Log(Mean fish biomass (g) + 1)") +
  theme_light()

biobox.dredge.plot.size <- ggplot(data = biobox.dredge, aes(x = mean_live, 
                                                            y = log(size +1))) +
  geom_point(color = "#5EB1BF") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "#5EB1BF", fill = "#5EB1BF") +
  theme_classic() +
  xlab("Mean live oyster count") +
  ylab("Log(Mean fish size (mm) + 1)") +
  theme_light()


biobox.dredge.plots <- biobox.dredge.plot.abu + biobox.dredge.plot.biomass + biobox.dredge.plot.size
ggsave(biobox.dredge.plots, file = "plots/biobox.dredge.plots.png", width = 10.5, height = 6)
```

Invertebrate Metrics

```{r}
biobox.summary2 <- biobox %>%
  drop_na(species_type) %>%
  filter(species_type == "invertebrate") %>%
  group_by(bay, site, species_type, biobox_ID) %>%
  summarize(n = sum(number_individuals), biomass = sum(weight_g), size = mean(total_length_mm)) %>%
  ungroup()

biobox.dredge2 <- biobox.summary2 %>%
  inner_join(oysters.dredge) 

biobox.hydro2 <- biobox.summary2 %>%
  inner_join(hydro.sum)

#statistics - linear models, log transformed
mod_abu2 <- lm(log(n + 1) ~ mean_live, data = biobox.dredge2)
summary(mod_abu2) #significant

mod_biomass2 <- lm(log(biomass + 1) ~ mean_live, data = biobox.dredge2)
summary(mod_biomass2) #not significant


biobox.dredge.plot.abu2 <- ggplot(data = biobox.dredge2, aes(x = mean_live,
                                                             y = log(n+1))) +
  geom_point(color = "#7FB069") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "#7FB069", fill = "#7FB069") +
  theme_classic() +
  xlab("Mean live oyster count") +
  ylab("Log(Mean invertebrate abundance + 1)") +
  theme_light()

biobox.dredge.plot.biomass2 <- ggplot(data = biobox.dredge2, aes(x = mean_live, 
                                                                 y = log(biomass + 1))) +
  geom_point(color = "#7FB069") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "#7FB069", fill = "#7FB069") +
  theme_classic() +
  xlab("Mean live oyster count") +
  ylab("Log(Mean invertebrate biomass (g) + 1)") +
  theme_light()


biobox.dredge.plots2 <- biobox.dredge.plot.abu2 + biobox.dredge.plot.biomass2 

ggsave(biobox.dredge.plots2, file = "plots/biobox.dredge.plots2.png", width = 10.5, height = 6)
```

Salinity Map

```{r}
hydro.map.sum<- hydro_maps %>%
  group_by(site, bay, lat, lon) %>%
  filter(depth_recording == "bottom") %>%
  summarise(mean.temp = mean(water_temp_C),
            mean.sal = mean(sal_ppt))
oyster.hydro.map <- oysters.dredge %>%
  inner_join(hydro.map.sum)

# Convert the joined data frame into a spatial (sf) object
hydro.maps.spatial <- st_as_sf(
  oyster.hydro.map,
  coords = c("lon", "lat"), # Specify longitude and latitude columns to create point geometry
  crs = 4326  # Set coordinate reference system to WGS84 (standard lat/lon)
)

library(osmdata)

# Padding added around the data extent to avoid points touching map edges
lon_pad <- 0.2
lat_pad <- 0.25


# Define a bounding box based on sampling locations
# Expanded slightly using padding values
bbox <- c(
  xmin = min(hydro.map.sum$lon) - lon_pad,
  ymin = min(hydro.map.sum$lat) - lat_pad,
  xmax = max(hydro.map.sum$lon) + lon_pad,
  ymax = max(hydro.map.sum$lat) + lat_pad
)


# Download coastline line features from OpenStreetMap
#osm_coast <- opq(bbox = bbox) |>
  #add_osm_feature(key = "natural", value = "coastline") |>
  #osmdata_sf()


# Extract line geometries representing coastlines
# Ensure CRS matches the oyster point data
#coast_sf <- osm_coast$osm_lines |>
  #st_transform(4326)

#salinity.count.map <- ggplot() +
 # geom_sf(
  #  data = coast_sf,
   # color = "grey50",
    #linewidth = 0.3
  #) +
  #geom_sf(
   # data = hydro.maps.spatial,
    #aes(color = mean.sal, size = mean_live),
    #alpha = 0.9
  #) +
  #scale_color_viridis_c(name = "Mean Salinity (PSU)") +
  #scale_size_continuous(
   # name = "Live Oyster Count",
    #range = c(2, 6)
  #) +
  #coord_sf(
   # xlim = c(bbox["xmin"], bbox["xmax"]),
    #ylim = c(bbox["ymin"], bbox["ymax"]),
    #expand = FALSE
  #) +
  #theme_minimal() +
  #theme(panel.grid = element_blank()) +
  #labs(
   # title = "Mean Salinity and Live Oyster Abundance Across Texas Bays",
    #x = "Longitude",
    #y = "Latitude"
  #)

#salinity.count.map

#ggsave(salinity.count.map, file = "plots/salinity.count.map.png", width = 10.5, height = 6)

```

Mapview - Salinity Map and Temperature

```{r}
library(mapview)

hydro.maps.spatial <- st_as_sf(
  oyster.hydro.map,
  coords = c("lon", "lat"),
  crs = 4326  # WGS84
)

mapview(
  hydro.maps.spatial,
  xcol = "Oyster Sites",  ycol = "Mean Salinity",
  col.regions = viridisLite::viridis(100),
  alpha.regions = 0.9,
  legend = TRUE)


hydro.maps.spatial$size_scaled <- scales::rescale(
  hydro.maps.spatial$mean_live,
  to = c(2, 8)
)

salinity.m <- mapview(
  hydro.maps.spatial,
  zcol = "mean.sal",
  cex  = hydro.maps.spatial$size_scaled,
  col.regions = viridisLite::plasma(100),
  alpha.regions = 0.9,
  legend = TRUE
)
salinity.m

temp.m <- mapview(
  hydro.maps.spatial,
  zcol = "mean.temp",
  cex  = hydro.maps.spatial$size_scaled,
  col.regions = viridisLite::plasma(100),
  alpha.regions = 0.9,
  legend = TRUE
)
temp.m
```

Temperature Map

```{r}
#temp.count.map <- ggplot() +
 # geom_sf(
  #  data = coast_sf,
   # color = "grey50",
    #linewidth = 0.3
  #) +
  #geom_sf(
   # data = hydro.maps.spatial,
    #aes(color = mean.temp, size = mean_live),
    #alpha = 0.9
  #) +
  #scale_color_viridis_c(name = "Mean Temperature (Celcius)", option = "plasma") +
  #scale_size_continuous(
   # name = "Live Oyster Count",
    #range = c(2, 6)
  #) +
  #coord_sf(
  #  xlim = c(bbox["xmin"], bbox["xmax"]),
  #  ylim = c(bbox["ymin"], bbox["ymax"]),
   # expand = FALSE
  #) +
  #theme_minimal() +
 # theme(panel.grid = element_blank()) +
#  labs(
    #title = "Mean Temperature and Live Oyster Abundance Across Texas Bays",
    #x = "Longitude",
   # y = "Latitude"
  #)

#temp.count.map
#ggsave(temp.count.map, file = "plots/temp.count.map.png", width = 10.5, height = 6)
```

RDA - Relative Abundance by Oyster Density

```{r}
library(ggrepel) 

comm_raw <- biobox %>%
  group_by(bay, site, species) %>%
  summarise(abundance = sum(number_individuals), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = species,
    values_from = abundance,
    values_fill = 0
  )
 
# Save site identifiers for later reattachment
site_ids <- comm_raw %>%
  select(bay, site)

# Remove non-numeric columns so matrix contains ONLY species data
comm_mat <- comm_raw %>%
  select(-bay, -site)

# Hellinger transform data:
# - Converts counts to relative abundances
# - Square-rooted to reduce dominance of abundant taxa
# - Makes data suitable for Euclidean-based methods like RDA
comm_hel <- decostand(comm_mat, method = "hellinger")


env <- hydro %>%
  group_by(bay, site) %>%
  summarise(
    temp = mean(water_temp_C, na.rm = TRUE),
    salinity = mean(sal_ppt, na.rm = TRUE),
    DO = mean(DO_mgL, na.rm = TRUE),
    pH = mean(pH, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

oysters_site <- oysters %>%
  filter(extraction_type == "Dredge", oyster_mortality == "live") %>%
  group_by(bay, site) %>%
  summarise(
    oyster_total = sum(oyster_total_count, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

#join datasets
env_full <- site_ids %>%
  left_join(oysters_site, by = c("bay", "site")) %>%
  left_join(env, by = c("bay", "site"))

# Constrained ordination:
# Species composition ~ environmental gradients
rda_mod <- rda(
  comm_hel ~ salinity + temp + DO + pH + oyster_total,
  data = env_full
)

# Displays:
# - Eigenvalues (variance explained)
# - Species–environment correlations
summary(rda_mod)

# Site scores:
# - Positions of each site in constrained ordination space
site_scores <- scores(rda_mod, display = "sites", scaling = 2)


# Environmental (biplot) scores:
# - These are the "loadings" of environmental variables
# - Arrow length = strength of correlation with ordination axes
env_scores  <- scores(rda_mod, display = "bp", scaling = 2)


# Species scores:
# - Location of species optima along gradients
species_scores <- scores(rda_mod, display = "species", scaling = 2)


plot_df <- data.frame(
  site_scores,
  oyster_density = factor(
    cut(
      env_full$oyster_total,
      breaks = c(-Inf, 50, Inf),
      labels = c("Low", "High")
    ),
    levels = c("Low", "High")
  )
)


# Identify species with strong contributions to RDA1–RDA2
# (distance from origin in ordination space)
species_strength <- rowSums(species_scores[,1:2]^2)

# Keep top 25% most influential species
top_species <- species_scores[species_strength > quantile(species_strength, 0.75), ]

# Prepare environmental vectors dataframe
env_df <- as.data.frame(env_scores)
env_df$var <- rownames(env_df)

RDA.plot<- ggplot(plot_df, aes(RDA1, RDA2, color = oyster_density)) +

  # Sites
  geom_point(size = 3.5, alpha = 0.9) +

  # Environmental vectors
  geom_segment(
    data = env_df,
    aes(x = 0, y = 0,
        xend = RDA1,
        yend = RDA2),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.3, "cm")),
    linewidth = 0.8,
    color = "black"
  ) +

  # Vector labels – pushed to arrow tips and repelled
  geom_text_repel(
    data = env_df,
    aes(x = RDA1 * 1.25, y = RDA2 * 1.25, label = var),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    fontface = "bold",
    size = 4,
    color = "black",
    segment.color = NA
  ) +

  # Species (still subtle)
  geom_text(
    data = as.data.frame(top_species),
    aes(RDA1, RDA2, label = rownames(top_species)),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 2.7,
    color = "grey40",
    alpha = 0.7
  ) +

  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "Low" = "#99c1de",
      "High" = "#386fa4"
    )
  ) +

  labs(
    x = "RDA1",
    y = "RDA2",
    color = "Live oyster counts"
  ) +

  coord_equal() +
  theme_classic(base_size = 15)
RDA.plot
ggsave(RDA.plot, file = "plots/RDA.plot.png", width = 10.5, height = 6)
```

```{r}

plot_df <- data.frame(
  site_scores,
  salinity = env_full$salinity
)


RDA.plot2<- ggplot(plot_df, aes(RDA1, RDA2, color = salinity)) +

  # Sites
  geom_point(size = 3.5, alpha = 0.9) +

  # Environmental vectors
  geom_segment(
    data = env_df,
    aes(x = 0, y = 0,
        xend = RDA1,
        yend = RDA2),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.3, "cm")),
    linewidth = 0.8,
    color = "black"
  ) +

  # Vector labels – pushed to arrow tips and repelled
  geom_text_repel(
    data = env_df,
    aes(x = RDA1 * 1.25, y = RDA2 * 1.25, label = var),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    fontface = "bold",
    size = 4,
    color = "black",
    segment.color = NA
  ) +

  # Species (still subtle)
  geom_text(
    data = as.data.frame(top_species),
    aes(RDA1, RDA2, label = rownames(top_species)),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 2.7,
    color = "grey40",
    alpha = 0.7
  ) +

  labs(
    x = "RDA1",
    y = "RDA2",
    color = "Salinity"
  ) +

  coord_equal() +
  theme_classic(base_size = 15)
RDA.plot2

```
